description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a weathered, light-colored stucco or plaster, showing signs of age and some surface wear. The house features a symmetrical arrangement of windows on both stories, with simple rectangular frames and no visible decorative trim. The front facade includes a single, centered entry door, flanked by windows, and there are no visible porches, steps, or overhangs. The roof has a few patches and a skylight or vent, indicating practical, utilitarian design. The overall appearance suggests a modest, older residential building typical of early American or Colonial styles.
description: A two-story, rectangular house with a steep gray gable roof, weathered light stucco walls, and symmetrical windows. The front features a simple entry door and no porch or decorative elements.
description_view_0: End view showing the steep gable roof, two vertically aligned windows, and plain stucco wall.
description_view_1: Front view with the main entry door centered between two sets of windows on each floor, and the full width of the house visible.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, mirroring view 0, with the same gable roof and window arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a similar window arrangement as the front but without a visible entry door.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple, centered door with no porch, steps, or decorative elements
